feature.title: SSO Integration
feature.description: Add single sign-on authentication to our SaaS platform, allowing users to log in using their corporate identity provider (Azure AD, Okta, or Google Workspace) instead of maintaining separate credentials.
target_users: End users of the SaaS platform

value_estimates.subcategory: Operational cost reduction → Support/incident cost reduction
value_estimates.reasoning: Password reset tickets currently account for 25-30% of IT helpdesk volume. SSO eliminates this category entirely for integrated accounts. Based on industry average of $15-25 per password reset ticket and 8,000-10,000 resets/year.
value_estimates.methodology: Bottom-up calculation: (annual reset tickets) × (cost per ticket). Validated against Gartner benchmark of $70 per password reset when including productivity loss.
assumptions: Current helpdesk handles approximately 8,000-10,000 password resets per year
SSO adoption reaches 80% within 6 months of launch

citations.title: Gartner: The True Cost of Password Resets (2025)
citations.excerpt: The average cost of a single password reset, including IT labor and end-user productivity loss, ranges from $40 to $70 per incident.

citations.title: Q3 2025 IT Helpdesk Report
citations.excerpt: Password-related tickets represented 28% of total helpdesk volume (2,340 of 8,357 tickets) in Q3 2025.

value_estimates.reasoning: SSO reduces login friction, which is a leading indicator for increased daily active usage. Industry data suggests 20-30% improvement in login completion rates after SSO implementation.
value_estimates.methodology: Comparative analysis of pre/post SSO adoption metrics from similar SaaS platforms.
assumptions: Current login abandonment rate is approximately 8-12%
SSO will reduce abandonment to under 3%

citations.title: Okta: The Business Value of SSO (2024)
citations.excerpt: Organizations implementing SSO see an average 27% increase in application adoption and a 50% reduction in login-related support requests.

value_estimates.subcategory: Security risk reduction → Reduced breach likelihood
value_estimates.reasoning: SSO with MFA reduces credential-based attack vectors. The expected value of risk reduction is estimated as (probability of credential breach) × (average breach cost) × (reduction factor from SSO+MFA).
value_estimates.methodology: Expected value calculation using IBM/Ponemon breach cost data and NIST probability estimates.
assumptions: Annual probability of credential-based breach estimated at 5-15%
SSO with MFA reduces credential attack surface by approximately 60-80%

citations.title: IBM Cost of a Data Breach Report 2025
citations.excerpt: The average cost of a data breach reached $4.88 million in 2025, with compromised credentials being the most common initial attack vector at 16% of breaches.

citations.title: Email from CISO regarding security audit findings
citations.excerpt: Our penetration test identified credential stuffing as a high-risk vector. Recommend SSO with enforced MFA as primary mitigation.

leading_indicators.linked_outcome: Increased platform engagement drives upsell and expansion revenue
leading_indicators.expected_impact: 15-25% increase in DAU within 3 months of SSO rollout

leading_indicators.linked_outcome: Reduced friction directly improves trial-to-paid conversion
leading_indicators.expected_impact: Login completion rate improves from ~90% to >97%

strategic_value.summary: SSO integration is a table-stakes enterprise requirement. Without it, the platform cannot compete for enterprise deals above 500 seats. Implementation unblocks the enterprise sales pipeline and positions the platform for SOC 2 Type II compliance, which is a prerequisite for 3 pending deals worth $2.1M ARR.
dimensions: Competitive differentiation — feature parity with enterprise competitors
Platform optionality — enables future SCIM provisioning and zero-trust architecture
Ecosystem leverage — partner integrations require SSO as a baseline

assumptions_made: Implementation covers Azure AD, Okta, and Google Workspace as initial identity providers
Existing user accounts will be migrated via email-based matching during SSO onboarding
SSO will be mandatory for enterprise tier but optional for self-serve tier
